seed_rate.notes: Ensure seed viability. Treat seeds with Rhizobium culture before sowing to promote nodulation.

sowing.soil_conditions: Well-drained soil with a fine seed bed. Soybean is very sensitive to deep planting and waterlogging.

irrigation.notes: Moist soil is necessary for uniform germination. Avoid flooding to prevent crusting.
Water stress at flowering reduces the number of flowers and pods per plant.
Most critical stage for yield. Water deficiency leads to shriveled seeds and premature maturity.

fertilizer.method: Band placement below the seed at sowing
fertilizer.notes: As a nitrogen-fixing legume, soybean needs only a starter dose of Nitrogen (20 kg/ha). Adequate Phosphorus and Sulfur (20 kg/ha) are critical for root/pod growth and oil synthesis.

maturity_indicators: Leaves turn yellow, dry, and drop off
Pods turn brown or straw-colored and make a rattling sound when shaken
Moisture level in grain drops to 14-16%
harvest.harvest_method: Sickle manual cutting or mechanical combine harvester
harvest.notes: Harvest promptly when mature to prevent pod shattering. Store at 10-12% moisture.
